tally_notes.P_patterns_source: Agents 1-12 findings classified by error pattern type. P3 (omission) dominates at 50.7% of all findings, consistent with PART2 being an implementation guide that defers detail to SOT references.
tally_notes.I_patterns_source: Session 17 IMP-A~F scripts. All 6 IMP scripts PASS in v13 re-scoping. I-patterns tallied from warnings and known issues documented in IMP results.
tally_notes.PR_patterns_source: Agent 10 prompt verification. PR1=CostGate LOCK violation in P-04, PR2=Matryoshka dim propagation, PR3=S7E-011 instruction hierarchy absent, PR4=CostGate inconsistent between P-04 and P-11, PR5=no prompt hierarchy reference.

session_17_detail.note: 334 structural items + 151 implementation facts = 485 (session 17 adjusted to 459 after removing overlapping Phase 0 cross-match baseline items already counted in CM)

session_20_detail.note: Agent 9 CI/CD+Migration, Agent 10 prompt Dim-D, Agent 11 traceability, Agent 12 meta-evaluation

session_21_detail.note: 42 formal challenges + 52 spot-checks on unchallenged findings

items_breakdown.computation: 459 + 132 + 116 + 85 + 52 = 844 raw, de-duplicated to ~791 after removing cross-session overlapping checks

findings.description: CostGate threshold in V0-STEP-4 AI prompt (L1097) uses 70%/85%/95% (P30-058 3-stage) contradicting D2.0-07 LOCK 80%/100% (2-stage). Same V0-STEP-4 description section (L1009) has correct values. Internal contradiction that would cause AI to generate non-compliant code.
findings.evidence: PART2 L1097 vs D2.0-07 §4.2 LOCK vs PART2 L1009
findings.action: URGENT: Replace L1097 values with '80% warn, 100% block (D2.0-07 §4.2 LOCK 2단계)'

findings.description: V2 Guardrails layer count: PART2 config L1339 correctly says V2=3-Layer, but V2-Phase 3 table (L675) says 4-Layer. CLAUDE.md §11 confirms V2=3-Layer.
findings.evidence: PART2 L1339 vs L675 vs CLAUDE.md §11
findings.action: Update L675 V2-Phase 3 table to say 3-Layer

findings.description: QoD < 0.4 storage restriction from D2.0-06 §2.5.2 not reflected in PART2. Without this threshold, L2/L3 storage may accept low-quality data.
findings.action: Add QoD threshold check to V1-Phase 2 storage admission logic

findings.description: V2 Lead Agent model upgrade to Opus not stated in PART2 V2 section.
findings.action: Add Lead=Opus note to V2 section header

findings.description: V3 L4_SELF_EVOLVING autonomy level has no SOT definition. D2.0-07 defines only L0-L3.
findings.evidence: D2.0-07 §3.2: L0-L3 only. PART2 L108: L4_SELF_EVOLVING
findings.action: Add L4 definition to D2.0-07 or annotate as PART2-originated extension

findings.description: Hook names mismatch between PART2 §6.1.3 (useAutonomy/useLog) and PHASE_B2 §3.1 canonical names (useApproval/useConfig). Unresolved since v8.
findings.action: Align PART2 hook names with PHASE_B2 canonical

findings.description: Matryoshka dimension conflict: PART2 says 256dim, PHASE_B4 says 512dim. V1-Phase 1 prompt propagates PART2 value.
findings.evidence: PART2 L1645: 'Matryoshka 256dim LOCK' vs PHASE_B4: matryoshka_dim=512
findings.action: Resolve matryoshka_dim via D2.0-06 DEC-005 authoritative value

findings.description: L0 Session Memory TTL: PART2 composite rule (session_end OR 30 days) is defensible, but CLAUDE.md §15 still has 7-day value creating residual confusion.
findings.evidence: PART2 L1907 vs D2.0-06 §2.1/§2.5.3 vs CLAUDE.md §15
findings.action: Update CLAUDE.md §15 L0 TTL to match PART2 composite rule

tier_1_urgent.description: HIGH severity requiring immediate PART2 correction before V0-STEP-4 execution

tier_2_important.description: MEDIUM severity — internal inconsistencies, SOT gaps, and naming conflicts needing resolution before affected version implementation

tier_3_informational.description: LOW severity — minor precision issues, V2/V3 future-scope items, acceptable by-reference omissions, and positive observations
tier_3_informational.detail: Includes 12 agent-cross duplicates already counted under canonical IDs, ~15 V2/V3 future-scope items appropriately deferred, ~12 by-reference omissions that are intentional PART2 design, and ~15 minor naming/precision issues

tier_4_dismissed.description: FALSE POSITIVES identified by adversarial review
tier_4_dismissed.additional: Plus 2 partial false positives on concurrency params (present in §7.5.3 L6178)

key_findings_summary.finding: F-A5-01: CostGate LOCK violation in V0-STEP-4 prompt — 70/85/95 vs SOT 80/100
key_findings_summary.impact: AI-generated code would use wrong cost thresholds
key_findings_summary.fix_effort: 5 minutes — single line edit in PART2 L1097

key_findings_summary.finding: F-A2-02: V2 Guardrails 4-Layer vs 3-Layer internal inconsistency
key_findings_summary.impact: V2 implementer confusion on guardrails scope
Low-quality data could enter L2/L3 storage without guard
key_findings_summary.fix_effort: 15 minutes — add QoD check to V1-Phase 2 storage section

key_findings_summary.finding: F-A2-05: V2 Lead Agent Opus upgrade unstated
key_findings_summary.impact: V2 implementer may not upgrade Lead model
key_findings_summary.fix_effort: 5 minutes — add note to V2 section

key_findings_summary.finding: F-A10-02: Matryoshka dim 256 vs 512 SOT conflict
key_findings_summary.impact: Wrong embedding dimension would cause search quality degradation
key_findings_summary.fix_effort: 10 minutes — check D2.0-06 DEC-005, update losing document

adversarial_summary.note: Overturn rate of 16.7% slightly exceeds 15% threshold. All 7 overturned findings were legitimate false positives (performance benchmarks exist in §7.5.3, CostGate double-counted, etc.) — the adversarial process correctly identified agent errors rather than masking real issues.

cross_phase_comparison.trend: Phase 1 (8 HIGH) -> Phase 2 (0 HIGH, fixes applied) -> Phase 3 (1 HIGH remaining). Verification depth increases (16->45->85) while severity decreases. Pipeline demonstrates effective self-correction.
